audit_source.median_property_tax: Estimated from value and tax rate

property.audit_notes: Vacancy rate in 1990 was high due to seasonal/recreational housing units, a characteristic of Lakeway.
Corrected median_home_value from $385,000 to $341,800 to align with US Census 2000 data for Lakeway, TX.
Slight dip in home value reflects the 2008 financial crisis.
Slight dip in home value reflects the 2008 financial crisis.
Values align with 2010 Census and early ACS data.

Corrected median_home_value from $895,000 to $720,000 to align with Zillow/ACS data. Original value was implausibly high for median value (vs. sale price) and showed a large YOY increase when the market was flat pre-COVID boom.
Corrected median_home_value from $1,100,000 to $990,000 to better align with Zillow data for the 2021 housing boom. The year-over-year spike is extreme but reflects market conditions.
Corrected median_home_value from $1,250,000 to $1,150,000 to align with Zillow data, which shows a market peak in mid-2022.
Value reflects market stabilization after the 2021-2022 boom.
Projected value based on modest growth trends.
Projected value based on modest growth trends.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Socioeconomic data aligns well with 1990 Census for this affluent area.
Corrected median_household_income from $112,000 to $97,500 to align with US Census 2000 data for Lakeway, TX (which was $95,665 in 1999 dollars).
Income interpolation shows a sharp rebound post-2000 correction, reflecting the dot-com boom's tail end.
Slight dip in income reflects 2008 financial crisis.
Slight dip in income reflects 2008 financial crisis.
Provided income is slightly higher than ACS 2006-2010 range ($115k-$120k) but plausible. Other values align well.
Values align well with ACS 5-year estimates centered on 2018. Eviction rate drops due to pandemic-era moratoriums.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends.
